# ecommerce-sql-analysis
# 🛒 E-Commerce User Behavior, funnel & Revenue Analysis

## 📖 Project Overview

This project analyzes customer behavior throughout the e-commerce purchasing journey using event-level user data. The objective is to identify conversion bottlenecks, evaluate marketing channel performance, and determine the products contributing the most revenue.

### Business Objectives

* Analyze user progression through the sales funnel.
* Identify stages with the highest customer drop-off.
* Measure revenue generated by each traffic source.
* Identify top-performing products.
* Provide actionable recommendations to improve conversion and revenue.

## 🛠️ Tools & Technologies

* SQL (SQLite / PostgreSQL)
* Python (Pandas)
* Git & GitHub

## 📂 Dataset Description

The dataset contains user interaction events from an e-commerce platform.

### Key Columns

| Column         | Description                      |
| -------------- | -------------------------------- |
| event_id       | Unique event identifier          |
| user_id        | Unique customer identifier       |
| event_type     | User action performed            |
| product_id     | Product associated with purchase |
| traffic_source | Acquisition channel              |
| amount         | Purchase value                   |
| event_time     | Timestamp of event               |

### Event Types

* Page View
* Add to Cart
* Checkout Start
* Payment Information
* Purchase

---

# Analysis 1: Sales Funnel Performance

### Business Question

How efficiently are users progressing through the purchasing journey?

### Results

| Stage               | Users |
| ------------------- | ----: |
| Page View           | 5,000 |
| Add to Cart         | 1,553 |
| Checkout Start      | 1,103 |
| Payment Information |   899 |
| Purchase            |   826 |

### Conversion Rates

| Conversion Stage          |   Rate |
| ------------------------- | -----: |
| View → Cart               | 31.06% |
| Cart → Checkout           | 71.02% |
| Checkout → Payment        | 81.50% |
| Payment → Purchase        | 91.88% |
| Overall Funnel Conversion | 16.52% |

### Key Findings

* The largest drop-off occurs between Page View and Add to Cart.
* Nearly 69% of visitors leave without adding products to their cart.
* Users who reach the payment stage have a very high probability of completing their purchase.

### Recommendations

* Improve product page design.
* Add customer reviews and trust signals.
* Test promotional offers and discounts.
* Optimize product descriptions and images.

---

# Analysis 2: Revenue by Traffic Source

### Business Question

Which acquisition channels generate the highest revenue?

### Results

| Traffic Source | Revenue ($) | Purchasing Users | Avg Order Value |
| -------------- | ----------: | ---------------: | --------------: |
| Organic        |   37,279.98 |              343 |          108.69 |
| Paid Ads       |   21,487.54 |              204 |          105.33 |
| Email          |   17,876.75 |              177 |          101.00 |
| Social         |   11,330.84 |              102 |          111.09 |

### Key Findings

* Organic traffic generates the highest revenue.
* Social traffic produces the highest average order value.
* Paid advertising contributes significant revenue volume.

### Recommendations

* Continue investing in SEO and organic growth.
* Test scaling social campaigns targeting high-value customers.
* Improve email segmentation to increase revenue per user.

---

# Analysis 3: Product Performance

### Business Question

Which products generate the highest revenue?

### Results

| Product ID | Revenue ($) |
| ---------- | ----------: |
| 205        |   15,905.07 |
| 404        |   15,377.31 |
| 101        |   14,688.18 |
| 305        |   14,622.48 |
| 201        |   13,925.94 |

### Key Findings

* Products 205 and 404 are the strongest revenue generators.
* Revenue distribution among top products is relatively balanced.
* No single product dominates total sales.

### Recommendations

* Increase visibility of top-performing products.
* Create product bundles around high-performing items.
* Monitor inventory levels for key revenue drivers.

---

# Conclusion

This analysis identified a strong overall funnel conversion rate of 16.5%, with the primary opportunity for improvement occurring at the Add-to-Cart stage. Organic traffic is the largest revenue driver, while Social traffic delivers the highest-value customers. Product revenue is well diversified across multiple products, reducing dependency on a single item.

The findings provide actionable recommendations to improve customer acquisition, conversion rates, and overall revenue performance.
